stemHtml:
A pilot flies an approach in hazy weather conditions, and has some difficulties recognising the runway features. Due to the visual conditions, the pilot has the perception of being farther, i.e, at a greater distance, from the runway than he/she actually is.

What is the illusion, and which danger is associated with it?

options.a: Surface planes: the pilot tends to fly turns because he/she fails to identify the runway features.
options.b: Atmospheric perspective: the pilot tends to fly lower and he/she can fly into terrain.
options.c: Size constancy: the pilot tends to hold altitude while he/she should be descending.
options.d: Autokinesis: the pilot tends to fly the aircraft towards a single light reference.
